metadata.title: Pokemon Pokopia (ぽこあポケモン / ぽこポケ) - Complete Habitat Data from Japanese Sources
metadata.description: Cross-verification data collected from Japanese game guide sites for habitat research

game_info.japanese_title: ぽこ あ ポケモン

habitat_system_overview.description_ja: 様々なオブジェクトの組み合わせでポケモンの「生息地」を作り出すことで新たなポケモンが登場する
habitat_system_overview.description_en: Create habitats by placing specific combinations of grass, trees, rocks, furniture, and other items near each other to attract Pokemon

mechanics.discovery: Find Pokemon traces (sparkling areas with rainbow aura) while exploring islands to learn habitat requirements
mechanics.building: Place required items near each other - exact positions from Habitat Dex are not strictly required, but all specified items must be within a certain range
mechanics.pokemon_appearance: After building a habitat, Pokemon appear after a certain time has passed
mechanics.conditions: Some Pokemon require specific weather (sunny/rainy) or time of day (day/night) conditions
mechanics.befriending: When a Pokemon starts living in a habitat, it becomes their home; players can befriend them
mechanics.hints: Learn habitat requirements by: examining Pokemon traces, speaking with Pokemon, purchasing clues from Pokemon Center PC

cloud_island_exclusives.note: 4 habitats can only be built on Cloud Islands

habitats.serebii_description: Four tufts of tall grass bunched together in a plot. The perfect hiding place for small Pokémon
Shaded tall grass that stays cool, even in the midday sun. Pokémon might stop by to relax

habitats.pokemon: Professor Tangrowth (はかせモジャンボ)
Peakychu / Delivery Pikachu (おとどけピカチュウ)
Smearguru / Artist Smeargle (えかきドーブル)
Mosslax / Moss Snorlax (コケカビゴン)

cross_reference_notes.numbering_discrepancy: Some sources list habitat 210 (ハイなロックステージ / Amped rock stage) separately, while Serebii's list shows 189 as Amped rock stage and 190 as Low-key rock stage. The Gamepedia tools source lists up to 214. The numbering may differ slightly between sources.
cross_reference_notes.incomplete_data: Habitats 133-209 have Japanese names and item requirements from Japanese sources, but Pokemon spawn data was not fully available from the fetched pages for these higher-numbered habitats. The English Game8 page data was truncated at habitat 132.
cross_reference_notes.under_investigation: Several habitats are marked as '調査中' (under investigation) in Japanese sources, indicating the community is still documenting them: 146, 148, 154, 156, 173, 187, 195, 198, 200
cross_reference_notes.event_habitats: 3 event habitats (E001-E003) exist but have limited documentation. E003 appears to be タンポポとお昼ご飯 (Dandelion lunchtime) based on one source.
cross_reference_notes.serebii_vs_gamepedia: Serebii lists 209 standard + 3 event = 212 total. The Gamepedia tools source lists items up to 214, possibly including unreleased or datamined habitats.
cross_reference_notes.data_quality_warning: Pokemon lists for habitats 1-132 are well-verified across multiple sources (Game8 EN, Game8 JP, GameWith, Pokeraku). Habitats 133-209 need additional cross-verification with Korean and English sources.
